author: Jelena Martinovic
headline: Yelp, Google Should Remove Illegal Weed Shops Says Los Angeles Initiative To Clean Up Cannabis Search Results
summary: Search engines and GPS platforms like Google and Apple Maps give legitimacy to unlicensed cannabis business in California by not distinguishing them from licensed dispensaries in respective search results. That's according to a measure that the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors approved this week in a unanimous vote. The proposal introduced by Supervisor Lindsey Horvath seeks to remove illegal cannabis dispensaries from Google, Yelp and other search engine platforms, reported Los Angeles Times.The measure also directs several county agencies to compile reports within three months on possible actions to curb unauthorized cannabis advertising in the unincorporated areas of the county. It also says five county leaders are to draft and sign a letter urging several major websites "to voluntarily stop advertising illegal cannabis dispensaries in the County."

author: Ananya Gairola
headline: Gavin Newsom Vetoes Controversial AI Regulation Bill-Backed By Elon Musk: Framework Must 'Keep Pace With The Technology'
summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom vetoed a bill to regulate AI, citing concerns that it could stifle innovation, despite acknowledging the need for public safety measures and support from figures like Elon Musk.

author: Ananya Gairola
headline: Mark Zuckerberg Says, Orion 'Is Probably Going To Be The Next Major Platform After Phones' — Meta's New AR Wearables Real-Life Tony Stark Glasses In The Making?
summary: In an interview with YouTuber Cleo Abram, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg described the Orion AR Glasses as the first-ever full holographic augmented reality glasses, developed over a decade, which he believes will become the next major computing platform after smartphones.

author: Ananya Gairola
headline: Mark Zuckerberg On Why He Wouldn't Want His Kids To Completely Rely On AI: 'There's Some Things That You Need To Able To Do Yourself'
summary: In an interview with YouTuber Cleo Abram, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg expressed both enthusiasm for AI's potential in children's education and concerns about over-reliance on technology, emphasizing the importance of maintaining fundamental skills like math and coding to foster independent thinking.
